records.ety: [OE. bringen, AS. bringan; akin to OS. brengian, D. brengen, Fries. brenga, OHG. bringan, G. bringen, Goth. briggan.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Brought (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Bringing.]

defs: To convey to the place where the speaker is or is to be; to bear from a more distant to a nearer place; to fetch.
quotes: And as she was going to fetch it, he called to her, and said, Bring me, I pray thee, a morsel of bread.
To France shall we convey you safe, And bring you back.

defs: To cause the accession or obtaining of; to procure; to make to come; to produce; to draw to.
quotes: There is nothing will bring you more honor . . . than to do what right in justice you may.

defs: To convey; to move; to carry or conduct.
quotes: In distillation, the water . . . brings over with it some part of the oil of vitriol.

defs: To persuade; to induce; to draw; to lead; to guide.
quotes: It seems so preposterous a thing . . . that they do not easily bring themselves to it.
The nature of the things . . . would not suffer him to think otherwise, how, or whensoever, he is brought to reflect on them.

defs: To produce in exchange; to sell for; to fetch; as, what does coal bring per ton?
notes: [v. i. by dropping the reflexive pronoun]
